revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{旧暦11月}} '''旧暦11月30日'''は[[11月 (旧暦)|旧暦11月]]の30日目である。年によっては11月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#仏滅|仏滅]]である。

== 誕生日 ==
== 忌日 == * [[元久]]元年（ユリウス暦[[1204年]][[12月22日]]） - [[藤原俊成]]、[[歌人]]（* [[1114年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==

[[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[zh:十一月三十]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]]

== 忌日 == * [[元久]]元年（ユリウス暦[[1204年]][[12月22日]]） - [[藤原俊成]]、[[歌人]]（* [[1114年]]） * [[天文 (日本)|天文]]14年（ユリウス暦[[1546年]][[1月2日]]） - [[妙玖]]、[[毛利元就]]の正室（*[[1499年]]）

'''November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)''' is the DAYX day of month MONTHY of the tradional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅}}.

== 忌日 == * [[元久]]元年（ユリウス暦[[1204年]][[12月22日]]） - [[藤原俊成]]、[[歌人]]（* [[1114年]]） * [[天文 (日本)|天文]]14年（ユリウス暦[[1546年]][[1月2日]]） - [[妙玖]]、[[毛利元就]]の正室（*[[1499年]]）

'''November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)''' is the DAYX day of month MONTHY of the tradional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅}}.
==OVERVIEW== {{Lunar November}} ''November 30'''<!--Lunar November30--> is the Nth day of [[11月 (旧暦)|Lunar November]]の30日目である。年によっては11月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#仏滅|仏滅]][[Category:仏滅]]である。

== Deaths == * [[元久]]元年（ユリウス暦[[1204年]][[12月22日]]） - [[藤原俊成]]、[[歌人]]（* [[1114年]]） * [[天文 (日本)|天文]]14年（ユリウス暦[[1546年]][[1月2日]]） - [[妙玖]]、[[毛利元就]]の正室（*[[1499年]]）

revision.text:
{{Lunar November}} '''November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} the 11th month of the tradional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅}}.
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.{{citation needed}}<!--taken from https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Chinese_calendar&oldid=1278636661 this is present on day 30 of all months, so add it to every single page here when you find a source for one of them-->

revision.text:
[[sn:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] [[zh:十一月三十]] [[en:draft:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar November}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}}.
As with all other day 30s in the Sino-Japanese calendar, its occurrence depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1204|ja|1204}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Fujiwara Toshinari|ja|藤原俊成}}, a waka poet, was dispatched (* {{ill|1114|ja|1114}}). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}14年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|January 2|ja|1月2日}}) – The empress’s consort, {{ill|Myōkyū|ja|妙玖}}, wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就}}, died (* {{ill|1499|ja|1499}}).
== Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|推古天皇}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|592|ja|592}} {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}) – (Note: In this context, this appears in the Deaths section of another page; here we assume it is a birth record for demonstration.)
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純}}, a Rinzai monk (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394}}). * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}}15年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友}}, the lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692}}). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}}2年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平}}, founder of the predecessor of the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利}}) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}}12年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The fourth generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北}}, a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755}}).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1128}}
[[Category:kk]][[en:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[sn:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]] [[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[sn:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[Category:Lunar November]] {{citation needed}} {{citation needed}} [[Category:Butsumetsu]] [[Category:Days 30 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[sn:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] [[zh:十一月三十]] [[en:draft:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:Untranslated Lunar Calendar Days]][[Category: Lunar Calendar Days]] [[sn:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] [[zh:十一月三十]] [[en:draft:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]

[[sn:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] [[zh:十一月三十]] [[en:draft:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar November}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. == Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1204|ja|1204}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Fujiwara Toshinari|ja|藤原俊成}}, a waka poet, was dispatched (* {{ill|1114|ja|1114}}). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}14年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|January 2|ja|1月2日}}) – The empress’s consort, {{ill|Myōkyū|ja|妙玖}}, wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就}}, died (* {{ill|1499|ja|1499}}).
== Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|推古天皇}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|592|ja|592}} {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}) – (Note: In this context, this appears in the Deaths section of another page; here we assume it is a birth record for demonstration.)
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純}}, a Rinzai monk (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394}}). * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}}15年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友}}, the lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692}}). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}}2年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平}}, founder of the predecessor of the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利}}) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}}12年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The fourth generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北}}, a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755}}).

[[sn:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] [[zh:十一月三十]] [[en:draft:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar November}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}}.
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明}}元年 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純}}, a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394}})<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}}15年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友}}, lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692}}). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}}2年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平}}, founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利}}) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}}12年 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北}}, a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755}}).

As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}}1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1204|ja|1204}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Fujiwara Toshinari|ja|藤原俊成}}, a waka poet, was dispatched (* {{ill|1114|ja|1114}}). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|January 2|ja|1月2日}}) – The empress’s consort, {{ill|Myōkyū|ja|妙玖}}, wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就}}, died (* {{ill|1499|ja|1499}}).
== Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|推古天皇}}1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|592|ja|592}} {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.)
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明}}1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純}}, a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394}})<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}}15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友}}, lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692}}). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}}2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平}}, founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利}}) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}}12th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北}}, a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755}}).

[[sn:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] [[zh:十一月三十]] [[en:draft:November 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar November}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}}.
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}}1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1204|ja|1204}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – {{ill|Fujiwara Toshinari|ja|藤原俊成}}, a waka poet, was dispatched (* {{ill|1114|ja|1114}}). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文}}14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546}} {{ill|January 2|ja|1月2日}}) – The empress’s consort, {{ill|Myōkyū|ja|妙玖}}, wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就}}, died (* {{ill|1499|ja|1499}}).
== Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|推古天皇}}1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|592|ja|592}} {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.)
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明}}1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純}}, a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394}})<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}}15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友}}, lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692}}). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}}2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}}) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平}}, founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利}}) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}}12th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北}}, a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755}}).

== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}} ) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純}} , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394}} )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友}} , lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692}} ). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}} ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利}} ) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653}} ). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 12th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北}} , a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755}} ).

== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}} ) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純}} , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394}} )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友}} , lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692}} ). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}} ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利}} ) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653}} ). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 12th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北}} , a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755}} ).

== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}} ) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純}} , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394}} )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友}} , lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692}} ). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}} ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利}} ) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653}} ). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 12th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北}} , a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755}} ).

Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .

== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}} ) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純}} , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394}} )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友}} , lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692}} ). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日}} ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利}} ) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653}} ). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 12th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}} ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北}} , a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755}} ).

[[sn:November 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] [[zh:十一月三十]] [[en:draft:November 30 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunisolar November}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|12=draft|12=draft}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1204|ja|1204|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara Toshinari|ja|藤原俊成|12=draft|12=draft}} , a waka poet, was dispatched (* {{ill|1114|ja|1114|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=draft|12=draft}} 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1546|ja|1546|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|January 2|ja|1月2日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The empress’s consort, {{ill|Myōkyū|ja|妙玖|12=draft|12=draft}} , wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|Mōri Motonari|12=draft|12=draft}} , died (* {{ill|1499|ja|1499|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|Empress Suiko|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|592|ja|592|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.)
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純|12=draft|12=draft}} , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394|12=draft|12=draft}} )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友|12=draft|12=draft}} , lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|12=draft|12=draft}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利|12=draft|12=draft}} ) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 12th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=draft|12=draft}} , a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|27|11|29|10|28|12|11|1128}}
[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]]
[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunisolar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar November]] [[Category:Butsumetsu]] [[Category:Days 30 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:Lunisolar]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:November 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 11월 30일]] [[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:十一月三十]] [[en:draft:November 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[en:11月30日 (旧暦)]]

== Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|Empress Suiko|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|592|ja|592|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.)
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunji|ja|文明|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1481|ja|1481|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ikkyū Sōjun|ja|一休宗純|12=draft|12=draft}} , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|ja|1394|12=draft|12=draft}} )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1731|ja|1731|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi|ja|徳川継友|12=draft|12=draft}} , lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|ja|1692|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1737|ja|1737|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 18|ja|12月18日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|12=draft|12=draft}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatomi|ja|三井高利|12=draft|12=draft}} ) (* {{ill|1653|ja|1653|12=draft|12=draft}} ). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 12th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1829|ja|1829|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=draft|12=draft}} , a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|ja|1755|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q841089]] Wikidata name: 30th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 30|qq=November 30}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|qid=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1204|1204]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Shunzei|Fujiwara Toshinari]] , a waka poet, was dispatched (* [[:en:1114|1114]] ). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|qq=|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:January 2|January 2]] ) – The empress’s consort, [[:en:Lady Myōkyū|Myōkyū]] , wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|ja|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Motonari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1156545}} , died (* [[:en:1499|1499]] ). == Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|Empress Suiko|1=Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko Tennō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:592|592]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]] ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.) == Deaths == * [[:en:Civilization|Bunji]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – [[:en:Ikkyū|Ikkyū Sōjun]] , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|qid=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|qid=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

[[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Takahira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三井八郎右衛門|1=Mitsui Hachirōemon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354250}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of [[:en:Mitsui Takatoshi|Mitsui Takatomi]] ) (* [[:en:1653|1653]] ). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyanin|qq=|1=Tsuruya Namboku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1675237}} , a kabuki dramatist (* [[:en:1755|1755]] ).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841089]] Wikidata name: 30th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 30|qq=November 30}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

[[:en:Mitsui Takatoshi|Mitsui Takatomi]] ) (* [[:en:1653|1653]] ). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyanin|1=Tsuruya Namboku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1675237}} , a kabuki dramatist (* [[:en:1755|1755]] ).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841089]] Wikidata name: 30th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 30|qq=November 30}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1204|1204]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Shunzei|Fujiwara Toshinari]] , a waka poet, was dispatched (* [[:en:1114|1114]] ). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:January 2|January 2]] ) – The empress’s consort, [[:en:Lady Myōkyū|Myōkyū]] , wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|ja|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Motonari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1156545}} , died (* [[:en:1499|1499]] ). == Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|Empress Suiko|1=Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:592|592]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]] ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.) == Deaths == * [[:en:Civilization|Bunji]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – [[:en:Ikkyū|Ikkyū Sōjun]] , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* [[:en:1394|1394]] )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1731|1731]] [[:en:January 5|January 5]] ) – [[:en:Tokugawa Tsugutomo|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi]] , lord of the Owari Domain (* [[:en:1692|1692]] ). * [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1737|1737]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Takahira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三井八郎右衛門|1=Mitsui Hachirōemon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354250}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of [[:en:Mitsui Takatoshi|Mitsui Takatomi]] ) (* [[:en:1653|1653]] ). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyanin|1=Tsuruya Namboku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1675237}} , a kabuki dramatist (* [[:en:1755|1755]] ).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841089]] Wikidata name: 30th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 30|qq=November 30}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

[[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Shunzei|Fujiwara Toshinari]] , a waka poet, was dispatched (* [[:en:1114|1114]] ). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:January 2|January 2]] ) – The empress’s consort, [[:en:Lady Myōkyū|Myōkyū]] , wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|ja|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Motonari|ja_comment=jawiki link Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:592|592]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]] ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.) == Deaths == * [[:en:Civilization|Bunji]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – [[:en:Ikkyū|Ikkyū Sōjun]] , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* [[:en:1394|1394]] )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1731|1731]] [[:en:January 5|January 5]] ) – [[:en:Tokugawa Tsugutomo|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi]] , lord of the Owari Domain (* [[:en:1692|1692]] ). * [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1737|1737]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Takahira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三井八郎右衛門|1=Mitsui Hachirōemon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354250}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of [[:en:Mitsui Takatoshi|Mitsui Takatomi]] ) (* [[:en:1653|1653]] ). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyanin|1=Tsuruya Namboku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1675237}} , a kabuki dramatist (* [[:en:1755|1755]] ).

[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] {{wikidata link|Q841089}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841089]] Wikidata name: 30th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 30|qq=November 30}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1204|1204]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Shunzei|Fujiwara Toshinari]] , a waka poet, was dispatched (* [[:en:1114|1114]] ). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:January 2|January 2]] ) – The empress’s consort, [[:en:Lady Myōkyū|Myōkyū]] , wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|ja|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Motonari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1156545}} , died (* [[:en:1499|1499]] ). == Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|Empress Suiko|1=Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:592|592]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]] ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.) == Deaths == * [[:en:Civilization|Bunji]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – [[:en:Ikkyū|Ikkyū Sōjun]] , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* [[:en:1394|1394]] )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1731|1731]] [[:en:January 5|January 5]] ) – [[:en:Tokugawa Tsugutomo|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi]] , lord of the Owari Domain (* [[:en:1692|1692]] ). * [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1737|1737]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Takahira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三井八郎右衛門|1=Mitsui Hachirōemon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354250}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of [[:en:Mitsui Takatoshi|Mitsui Takatomi]] ) (* [[:en:1653|1653]] ). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyanin|1=Tsuruya Namboku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1675237}} , a kabuki dramatist (* [[:en:1755|1755]] ).

[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月三十]] {{wikidata link|Q841089}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh_min_nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh_min_nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841089]] Wikidata name: 30th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 30|qq=November 30}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1204|1204]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Shunzei|Fujiwara Toshinari]] , a waka poet, was dispatched (* [[:en:1114|1114]] ). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:January 2|January 2]] ) – The empress’s consort, [[:en:Lady Myōkyū|Myōkyū]] , wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|ja|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Motonari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1156545}} , died (* [[:en:1499|1499]] ). == Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|Empress Suiko|1=Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:592|592]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]] ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.) == Deaths == * [[:en:Civilization|Bunji]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – [[:en:Ikkyū|Ikkyū Sōjun]] , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* [[:en:1394|1394]] )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1731|1731]] [[:en:January 5|January 5]] ) – [[:en:Tokugawa Tsugutomo|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi]] , lord of the Owari Domain (* [[:en:1692|1692]] ). * [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1737|1737]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Takahira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三井八郎右衛門|1=Mitsui Hachirōemon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354250}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of [[:en:Mitsui Takatoshi|Mitsui Takatomi]] ) (* [[:en:1653|1653]] ). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyanin|1=Tsuruya Namboku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1675237}} , a kabuki dramatist (* [[:en:1755|1755]] ).

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1204|1204]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – [[:en:Fujiwara no Shunzei|Fujiwara Toshinari]] , a waka poet, was dispatched (* [[:en:1114|1114]] ). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:January 2|January 2]] ) – The empress’s consort, [[:en:Lady Myōkyū|Myōkyū]] , wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|ja|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Motonari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1156545}} , died (* [[:en:1499|1499]] ). == Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|Empress Suiko|1=Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:592|592]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]] ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.) == Deaths == * [[:en:Civilization|Bunji]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – [[:en:Ikkyū|Ikkyū Sōjun]] , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* [[:en:1394|1394]] )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1731|1731]] [[:en:January 5|January 5]] ) – [[:en:Tokugawa Tsugutomo|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi]] , lord of the Owari Domain (* [[:en:1692|1692]] ). * [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1737|1737]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Takahira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三井八郎右衛門|1=Mitsui Hachirōemon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354250}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of [[:en:Mitsui Takatoshi|Mitsui Takatomi]] ) (* [[:en:1653|1653]] ). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyanin|1=Tsuruya Namboku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1675237}} , a kabuki dramatist (* [[:en:1755|1755]] ).

– The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyanin|1=Tsuruya Namboku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1675237}} , a kabuki dramatist (* [[:en:1755|1755]] ).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841089]] Wikidata name: 30th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 30|qq=November 30}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1204|1204]] (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1546|1546]] [[:en:January 2|January 2]] ) – The empress’s consort, [[:en:Lady Myōkyū|Myōkyū]] , wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|ja|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Motonari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1156545}} , died (* [[:en:1499|1499]] ). == Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|Empress Suiko|1=Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:592|592]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]] ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.) == Deaths == * [[:en:Civilization|Bunji]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – [[:en:Ikkyū|Ikkyū Sōjun]] , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* [[:en:1394|1394]] )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1731|1731]] [[:en:January 5|January 5]] ) – [[:en:Tokugawa Tsugutomo|Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi]] , lord of the Owari Domain (* [[:en:1692|1692]] ). * [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1737|1737]] [[:en:December 18|December 18]] ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Takahira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三井八郎右衛門|1=Mitsui Hachirōemon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354250}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of [[:en:Mitsui Takatoshi|Mitsui Takatomi]] ) (* [[:en:1653|1653]] ). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:December 22|December 22]] ) {{Not to be confused with|November 30|qq=November 30}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月30日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 30일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月三十]][[zh:十一月三十]]
{{Not to be confused with|November 30|qq=November 30}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day‑30 occurrences in the Sino‑Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino‑Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events == * {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1204|en|1204|WD=Q5228|lt=1204}} {{ill|December 22|en|December 22|WD=Q2636|lt=December 22}} ) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Shunzei|en|Fujiwara no Shunzei|WD=Q934255|lt=Fujiwara Toshinari}} , a waka poet, was dispatched (* {{ill|1114|en|1114|WD=Q19533|lt=1114}} ). * {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1546|en|1546|WD=Q6533|lt=1546}} {{ill|January 2|en|January 2|WD=Q2151|lt=January 2}} ) – The empress’s consort, {{ill|Lady Myōkyū|en|Lady Myōkyū|WD=Q10796126|lt=Myōkyū}} , wife of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|ja|Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari|2=ja|3=毛利元就|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Motonari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1156545}} , died (* {{ill|1499|en|1499|WD=Q6708|lt=1499}} ). == Births == * {{ill|Suiko Tennō|ja|Empress Suiko|1=Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592}} {{ill|December 12|en|December 12|WD=Q2369|lt=December 12}} ) – (Note: This birth record appears as a placeholder.) == Deaths == * {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|WD=Q8432|lt=Bunji}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1481|en|1481|WD=Q6631|lt=1481}} {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18}} ) – {{ill|Ikkyū|en|Ikkyū|WD=Q467613|lt=Ikkyū Sōjun}} , a Rinzai monk and founder of the Nichiren sect (* {{ill|1394|en|1394|WD=Q6398|lt=1394}} )<ref>Reference: Encyclopedia Britannica</ref> * {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1731|en|1731|WD=Q6963|lt=1731}} {{ill|January 5|en|January 5|WD=Q2202|lt=January 5}} ) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsugutomo|en|Tokugawa Tsugutomo|WD=Q1052927|lt=Tokugawa Tsuguyoshi}} , lord of the Owari Domain (* {{ill|1692|en|1692|WD=Q7685|lt=1692}} ). * {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1737|en|1737|WD=Q6993|lt=1737}} {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18}} ) – {{ill|Mitsui Takahira|ja|三井高平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Takahira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三井八郎右衛門|1=Mitsui Hachirōemon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354250}} , founder of the precursor to the Mitsukoshi department store (child of {{ill|Mitsui Takatoshi|en|Mitsui Takatoshi|WD=Q6883251|lt=Mitsui Takatomi}} ) (* {{ill|1653|en|1653|WD=Q6965|lt=1653}} ). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 12th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829}} {{ill|December 22|en|December 22|WD=Q2636|lt=December 22}} ) – The fourth-generation head of {{ill|Tsuruyanin|ja|鶴屋南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyanin|1=Tsuruya Namboku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1675237}} , a kabuki dramatist (* {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755}} ).

{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . Its Rokuyō is {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and its day is {{ill|Tomobiki|WD=Q96759840|1=tomobiki}}{{efn|ja|友引|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}} .
As with all other day-30 occurrences in the Sino-Japanese calendar, its appearance depends on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
As with all other day 30s of the Sino-Japanese calendar, this occurrence sometimes happens and sometimes does not, depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
